description: Implement Input/Output S3 buckets and EventBridge rule using strict TDD. Write failing tests first, then implement minimal CDK code to make them pass. Update ARCHITECTURE.md to reflect the new resources.
steps: Step 1 - Write failing tests in tests/unit/test_cdk_base_stack.py (TDD RED phase): Add tests that assert: (a) An S3 bucket 'SleepAudioInputBucket' exists with versioning enabled, SSE-S3 or KMS encryption, public access blocked, and EventBridge notifications enabled. (b) An S3 bucket 'SleepAudioOutputBucket' exists with versioning enabled, encryption, and public access blocked. (c) An EventBridge rule exists that matches S3 Object Created events from the input bucket. (d) The EventBridge rule has a target (placeholder). Use aws_cdk.assertions Template.has_resource_properties() and Template.resource_count_is() methods. Follow the pattern in the existing test file.

Step 2 - Remove or update the existing test_stack_has_no_unexpected_resources test since the stack will now have resources. Replace it with a test that verifies the expected resource count (2 S3 buckets, 1 EventBridge rule, etc.).
Step 3 - Run tests to confirm they FAIL (RED phase): NODE_OPTIONS='' pytest tests/ -v

Step 4 - Implement the stack code in cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py (GREEN phase): Add the S3 input bucket with: versioning enabled, S3-managed encryption (SSE-S3 or KMS), block all public access, event_bridge_enabled=True. Add the S3 output bucket with: versioning enabled, encryption, block all public access. Add an EventBridge Rule that matches on {source: ['aws.s3'], detail-type: ['Object Created'], detail: {bucket: {name: [input_bucket.bucket_name]}}}. Add a placeholder target (e.g., a CloudWatch LogGroup or an events.targets that is minimal). Use L2 constructs (aws_s3.Bucket, aws_events.Rule, etc.).
Step 5 - Run tests again to confirm they PASS (GREEN phase): NODE_OPTIONS='' pytest tests/ -v
Step 6 - Run cdk synth to verify the template: NODE_OPTIONS='' npx cdk synth

Step 7 - Update ARCHITECTURE.md: Refine the Mermaid diagram to show that the Input Bucket, Output Bucket, and EventBridge Rule are now implemented (mark them as implemented vs planned). Add a short 'Current Implementation Status' section listing what exists.
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass including new assertions for both S3 buckets and the EventBridge rule
cdk synth succeeds with no errors
Input bucket has versioning, encryption, public access blocked, and EventBridge notifications enabled
Output bucket has versioning, encryption, and public access blocked
EventBridge rule matches Object Created events from the input bucket
EventBridge rule has at least one target (placeholder is fine)
ARCHITECTURE.md is updated with current implementation status
The existing test_stack_synthesizes_valid_template test still passes
verification: NODE_OPTIONS='' pytest tests/ -v shows all tests passing
NODE_OPTIONS='' npx cdk synth exits with 0 and shows S3 bucket and Events Rule resources in output
findings: EventBridge notifications on S3 buckets are rendered as Custom::S3BucketNotifications resources in synthesized CloudFormation (not as a property on the bucket itself). Tests should assert the custom resource rather than a NotificationConfiguration bucket property.
